hero.headline: DPU-COL Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: NAAC A++ | NIRF #41 University 2025 | UGC-DEB Approved | Total fees ₹1,89,400

sections.tldr: DPU-COL Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management covers material management, warehouse management, global logistics, transportation and distribution, SCM strategy and design, and green supply chain. NAAC A++ NIRF #41. Total fee ₹1,89,400 with ₹10,000 discount. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. edX certifications bundled. UGC-DEB approved.

about.body:
DPU-COL's MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management covers the full supply chain lifecycle: material management (procurement, vendor development, inventory control), warehouse management (layout design, WMS, pick-pack-ship), global logistics (international freight, customs, Incoterms), transportation and distribution (fleet management, route optimisation, last-mile delivery), SCM strategy and design (network design, S&OP, demand planning), and green supply chain (sustainability metrics, carbon footprint reduction, circular economy).

The inclusion of green supply chain as a standalone subject is notable. With ESG reporting becoming mandatory for many Indian companies (BRSR requirements from SEBI), supply chain professionals who understand sustainability metrics and carbon footprint reduction are in growing demand.

India's logistics sector is undergoing transformation through PM Gati Shakti, the National Logistics Policy, and growing e-commerce fulfilment demand. This MBA positions graduates for management roles in this expanding sector.

Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune holds NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 (University 2025). edX certifications are bundled.

EdifyEdu verifies data independently and takes no commission from any university.

syllabus.note: Semesters 1-2 cover the common MBA management core. Semesters 3-4 below show the Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation subjects.

subjects.name: Material Management
subjects.desc: Procurement planning, vendor evaluation and development, ABC-XYZ inventory classification, purchase order management, stores management, and materials cost control.

subjects.name: Warehouse Management
subjects.desc: Warehouse layout design, WMS (Warehouse Management System) overview, pick-pack-ship processes, inventory accuracy (cycle counting), cold storage operations, and warehouse safety.

subjects.name: Global Logistics
subjects.desc: International freight forwarding, Incoterms 2020, customs clearance procedures, containerisation, multimodal transport, and free trade zone operations.

subjects.name: Transportation and Distribution
subjects.desc: Fleet management, route optimisation, transportation cost analysis, last-mile delivery models, distribution network design, and 3PL/4PL management.

subjects.name: SCM Strategy and Design
subjects.desc: Supply chain network design, S&OP (Sales and Operations Planning), demand planning and forecasting, supply chain risk management, and make-vs-buy decisions.

subjects.name: Green Supply Chain
subjects.desc: Sustainability in supply chain operations, carbon footprint measurement, reverse logistics, circular economy models, BRSR reporting for supply chain, and sustainable procurement.

subjects.name: Project Work
subjects.desc: Industry-linked supply chain project. Typically a warehouse optimisation study, logistics cost reduction analysis, or green supply chain implementation plan.

whoHires.body:
DPU-COL MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management graduates target roles across the logistics value chain:

**Industries:** E-commerce fulfilment, 3PL and 4PL providers, FMCG distribution, manufacturing supply chain, pharmaceutical logistics, cold chain, freight forwarding, and government logistics (railways, ports, NLP)

**Entry-level roles:** Supply Chain Coordinator, Warehouse Executive, Logistics Executive, Procurement Officer, Transportation Planner, Distribution Executive

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Supply Chain Manager, Warehouse Manager, Logistics Manager, Procurement Manager, Distribution Manager, S&OP Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Supply Chain, Head of Logistics, Chief Supply Chain Officer, Director of Procurement, Head of Warehouse Operations (multi-site)

**Employers:** E-commerce (Amazon India, Flipkart, Meesho fulfilment), 3PLs (Delhivery, DTDC, Blue Dart, Gati), FMCG (HUL, ITC, Nestle supply chain), auto (Tata Motors, Maruti, Mahindra supply chain), and pharma (Cipla, Sun Pharma cold chain).

**Note:** DPU-COL provides placement assistance for all MBA programmes.

skills.body:
The Logistics and Supply Chain Management specialisation builds skills across procurement, warehousing, transport, and strategy:

**Technical skills:**
- Procurement: vendor scorecard design, RFP/RFQ process management, purchase order automation, and supplier relationship management
- Warehouse Operations: layout optimisation (ABC slotting), WMS configuration basics, cycle count programme design, and warehouse KPI tracking (OTIF, pick accuracy, dock-to-stock)
- Transportation: route optimisation methodology, freight rate negotiation, TMS (Transport Management System) overview, and last-mile cost modelling
- SCM Strategy: network design modelling, S&OP meeting facilitation, demand forecast accuracy tracking, and supply chain risk register management

**Sustainability skills:**
- Green Supply Chain: Scope 1/2/3 emissions accounting for supply chain, carbon footprint per unit calculation, reverse logistics programme design, and BRSR supply chain disclosure preparation

**Soft skills:** Managing multi-site warehouse teams, negotiating with freight vendors and 3PL providers, presenting supply chain performance to C-suite, coordinating cross-functional S&OP meetings, and leading sustainability transformation in supply chain operations.

comparisons.body:
Comparing DPU-COL Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management against peer programmes:

**vs Chandigarh University Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** CU at ₹1,65,000 with NIRF #19. CU wins on NIRF rank and fee. DPU at ₹1,89,400 holds NAAC A++ (vs CU A+) and includes green supply chain as a standalone subject. For NIRF-sensitive employers, CU is stronger.

**vs LPU Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** LPU at approximately ₹2,00,000 with Coursera Plus. DPU saves ₹10,600 with stronger NAAC (A++ vs A+) and NIRF ranking.

**vs Symbiosis Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management:** Symbiosis at approximately ₹1,70,000 with NIRF #20. Symbiosis wins on NIRF rank. DPU wins on NAAC grade (A++ vs A).

**vs Amrita Online MBA Operations Management:** Amrita at ₹2,24,000 with NIRF #8 covers operations broadly but may not go as deep on warehouse management, global logistics, and green supply chain specifically. For candidates targeting supply chain and logistics roles specifically, DPU's dedicated spec is more directly relevant.

fees.body:
The DPU-COL Online MBA in Logistics and Supply Chain Management has a total programme fee of ₹1,89,400 for Indian students (USD 3,600 for international students).

**Semester-wise fee breakdown:**
- Semester 1: ₹50,000
- Semester 2: ₹50,000
- Semester 3: ₹45,000
- Semester 4: ₹44,400
- **Total: ₹1,89,400**

**Scholarship:** ₹10,000 off for full payment in one or two transactions within 15 days (effective fee ₹1,79,400).

**Payment options:**
- Zero-cost EMI: from ₹2,500/month directly through DPU-COL (no external NBFC, no interest)
- Semester-wise payments as above
- No application fee

**Bundled:** edX certifications included at no extra cost.

Fees are indicative. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com before payment.

certificate.body:
Graduates receive an MBA degree certificate with Logistics and Supply Chain Management as the specialisation, issued by Dr. D.Y. Patil Vidyapeeth, Pune (DPU-COL) under UGC-DEB 2020 online mode standards.

The degree carries NAAC A++ accreditation and NIRF #41 University rank. It is valid for government logistics and procurement roles, PSU supply chain positions, railways and port authority management, and private-sector logistics and supply chain employers. edX certifications supplement the credential. DigiLocker issuance is standard.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from DPU-COL Online MBA Logistics and Supply Chain Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Nikhil R.
items.liked: Warehouse Management subject was directly applicable — layout optimisation and WMS concepts improved my fulfilment centre operations at an e-commerce company
items.disliked: More e-commerce-specific content (dark store operations, quick commerce logistics, hyperlocal delivery) would make the programme more current
items.body: I manage warehouse operations at an e-commerce fulfilment centre. The warehouse management, transportation, and SCM strategy subjects were directly applicable. NAAC A++ and NIRF #41 were accepted by my employer for promotion eligibility. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month made it accessible.

items.name: Anita S.
items.liked: Green Supply Chain module is rare in online MBA programmes — sustainability metrics and BRSR reporting are becoming mandatory for large companies
items.disliked: Global Logistics could include more on multimodal logistics corridors in India (DMIC, Sagarmala) and NLP-specific initiatives
items.body: I work in supply chain planning at an FMCG company. The SCM strategy, green supply chain, and material management subjects strengthened my planning and sustainability reporting skills. The green supply chain subject was the differentiator — our company is implementing BRSR reporting and this content was directly applicable.

items.name: Deepak J.
items.liked: Transportation and Distribution module covered fleet management and route optimisation at an operational level — useful for my 3PL business
items.disliked: More coverage of digital supply chain (IoT-based tracking, digital twins, control tower) would update the programme for modern logistics
items.body: I run a regional 3PL company. The transportation, warehouse, and global logistics subjects helped me structure my operations more professionally for larger client pitches. NAAC A++ from NIRF #41 gave the credential weight in tender documentation. The ₹10,000 lump-sum discount and edX certifications added value.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover green supply chain and sustainability?
faqs.answer: Yes. Green Supply Chain is a standalone Semester 4 subject covering carbon footprint measurement, reverse logistics, circular economy, BRSR reporting for supply chain, and sustainable procurement. This is rare in online MBA programmes and directly relevant as SEBI-mandated BRSR sustainability reporting becomes standard for large Indian companies.

faqs.question: Is this MBA valid for government logistics and procurement roles?
faqs.answer: Yes. DPU-COL's MBA is UGC-DEB approved from a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 university. It satisfies the MBA requirement for railways logistics management, port authority positions, government procurement offices, and NLP (National Logistics Policy) implementation roles.

faqs.question: How does this differ from DPU's Operations Management MBA?
faqs.answer: Operations Management covers production planning, inventory management, TQM, and manufacturing-focused operations. Logistics and SCM covers warehousing, transportation, global logistics, procurement, and green supply chain. Choose Operations for manufacturing plant management. Choose Logistics and SCM for supply chain, distribution, and logistics management roles.

faqs.question: What is the total fee?
faqs.answer: ₹1,89,400 total (Sem 1: ₹50,000, Sem 2: ₹50,000, Sem 3: ₹45,000, Sem 4: ₹44,400). ₹10,000 scholarship for upfront payment within 15 days. Zero-cost EMI from ₹2,500/month. No application fee. edX bundled. Reconfirm at dypatilonline.com.

faqs.question: Does the programme cover e-commerce logistics?
faqs.answer: The warehouse management, transportation and distribution, and SCM strategy subjects cover fulfilment, distribution, and last-mile delivery which are core e-commerce logistics competencies. There is no standalone e-commerce logistics module, but the operational frameworks (WMS, route optimisation, 3PL management) apply directly to e-commerce fulfilment operations.

chooseIf: You target logistics, supply chain, or distribution management roles and need a NAAC A++ NIRF #41 MBA credential.
Green supply chain and sustainability reporting skills are relevant for your company's BRSR compliance.
₹1,89,400 with zero-cost EMI and edX certifications fits your budget.
You work in e-commerce fulfilment, 3PL, FMCG distribution, or manufacturing supply chain.
consider: NIRF top 20 matters — CU (NIRF #19) at ₹1,65,000 or Symbiosis (NIRF #20) at ₹1,70,000 are alternatives.
Your target is manufacturing plant management rather than supply chain — DPU's Operations Management spec is more relevant.
You need international trade and EXIM-specific content — DPU's International Business Management spec covers DGFT and customs depth.
